tata.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: FY2022-23 is consolidated while later years are standalone

sections.about: This metric measures the percentage of inputs sourced through sustainable sourcing procedures and is a BRSR Principle 2 Essential Indicator. It reflects whether procurement is governed by defined supplier codes, ESG criteria, responsible sourcing policies and assurance mechanisms. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers:
Tata, JSW and SAIL all reported 100% in FY2023 and FY2024, while Jindal Stainless reported 90% in both years. In FY2025, Tata, JSW and Jindal reported 100%, while SAIL dropped to 0% in the standardized dataset. Tata’s 100% is supported by supplier sign-off to the Business Associate Code of Conduct and a Responsible Supply Chain Policy covering applicable laws, anti-bribery, health and safety, human rights, environmental protection, asset safeguarding and conflict-of-interest avoidance; Tata further disclosed that 100% of suppliers in India commit to sustainable sourcing.

The metric is sensitive to definition. For integrated BF-BOF companies, “sustainable sourcing” can be reported as policy coverage across supplier onboarding rather than physical low-carbon or certified-green inputs. This explains why companies with very different steel routes and raw-material footprints can all report 100%. SAIL’s FY2025 narrative states that it incorporates ESG principles into sourcing, uses IMS-certified captive mines and imports coal from jurisdictions with stringent ESG standards, but the standardized value is 0%, indicating a possible conversion, response-field or quantification issue rather than a simple absence of procedures.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranked joint 1st at 100%, tied with JSW and Jindal Stainless; SAIL was the only laggard at 0%. Tata’s rank has effectively held steady across all three years, although the FY2023 basis is consolidated. The main management implication is that Tata is already at ceiling level on policy-coverage reporting; future differentiation will need to come from deeper proof points such as high-risk category coverage, supplier maturity bands, third-party audits, ResponsibleSteel-equivalent certifications, Scope 3 supplier decarbonisation engagement and contract-level ESG incentives.
sections.comparabilityNotes: The 100% values should not be read as equivalent supply-chain maturity across peers. In several BRSR disclosures, the metric appears to capture supplier-policy commitment rather than independently verified sustainable material content.
